LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS GMG JANITORIAL, INC. AND GINA GREGORI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE REGARDING UNCLEAN HANDS WHICH, IN ANY EVENT, REQUIRES THE TRIAL COURT TO WEIGH THE EVIDENCE ADDUCED AT TRIAL BASED ON ALL THE FACTS AND CIRCUMSTANCES IN THE CASE. DEFENDANTS' OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFFS' EVIDENCE CONCERNING WHAT THEY WERE TOLD AND INSTRUCTED BY JOSEFA RODRUIGUEZ ARE OVERRULED. THOSE STATEMENTS ARE NOT OFFERED FOR THEIR TRUTH BUT FOR THE FACT THAT THE INSTRUCTIONS WERE GIVEN. THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. DEFENDANTS FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CAL. RULE OF COURT 3.1350(B) BY NOT STATING EACH ISSUE SEPARATELY IN THE SAME WORDS IN THE NOTICE AND SEPARATE STATEMENT OR LISTING THE SPECIFIC MATERIAL FACTS SEPARATELY FOR EACH ISSUE. IN ANY EVENT, THERE WOULD BE TRIABLE ISSUES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON MAY-07-2013 IN 206, DEFENDANTS GMG JANITORIAL, INC. AND GINA GREGORI'S MOTION TO CONTINUIE TRIAL DATE. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT DENIED THE MOTION TO CONTINUE FOR THE REASONS PROPOUNDED BY THE DEFENSE. THE MOTION TO CONTINUE IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE WITH REFERENCE TO EFFORTS BY THE PARTIES TO SETTLE THE CASE. THE TRIAL DATE OF 5/20/13 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206 IS MAINTAINED. calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-05-2013 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-15-2013 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-23-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Continue Trial Date
Off Calendar May-07-2013 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-06-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Anne V. Leinfelder, Esq. of Anderies & Gomes, Tel. 415.217.8802, for defendants and moving party;
Un Kei Wu, Esq. of Liberation Law Group, Tel. 415.695.1000, for plaintiffs and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs GMG JANITORIAL, INC. and GINA GREGORI's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There is a triable issue regarding unclean hands which, in any event, requires the trial court to weigh the evidence adduced at trial based on all the facts and circumstances in the case. Defendants' objections to plaintiffs' evidence concerning what they were told and instructed by Josefa Rodruiguez are overruled. Those statements are not offered for their truth but for the fact that the instructions were given. The alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Defendants failed to comply with Cal. Rule of Court 3.1350(b) by not stating each issue separately in the same words in the notice and separate statement or listing the specific material facts separately for each issue. In any event, there would be triable issues. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-17-2013 Continued To May-06-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS GMG JANITORIAL, INC. AND GINA GREGORI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to May 6, 2013, on the Court's motion. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Plaintiffs Alejandro Mairena, Marta Melgar And Carlos Orellana'S Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Mar-21-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Un Kei Wu, Esq. of Liberation Law Group, Tel. 415.695.1000, for plaintiffs and moving party;
Anne V. Leinfelder, Esq. of Anderies & Gomes, Tel. 707.837.7310, for defendants and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS ALEJANDRO MAIRENA, MARTA MELGAR and CARLOS ORELLANA's Motion To Compel is Granted, in part. Within 10 days, Defendants shall provide further responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One to Gregori, nos. 19, 20, 22, 23, and Request for Production of Documents, Set Two to GMG, no. 22. The motion is denied in all other respects. Request for attorneys fees denied. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

Mtn To Compel
Off Calendar Mar-14-2013 Text Ruling
